title: Oreogrammitis nuda
intro: Oreogrammitis nuda, commonly known as the Naked Grammitid, is a species of fern native to the tropical regions of Australia. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Oreogrammitis nuda include flavonoids, terpenoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, including infections and skin disorders.
taxonomy: Oreogrammitis nuda belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. It is a species of fern in the genus Oreogrammitis. Oreogrammitis nuda is a member of the Leptochiloid clade of Polypodiaceae. The species is native to New Caledonia and has been classified based on its morphological characteristics. Further research may be necessary to confirm its classification within the fern family.
